items.title: Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn’t need a charging station
items.summary: The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.

items.title: Zepto’s IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody’s answered yet
items.summary: Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.

items.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
items.summary: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

items.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
items.summary: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

items.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
items.summary: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

items.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
items.summary: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

items.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
items.summary: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

items.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
items.summary: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

items.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
items.summary: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

items.title: Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for $220M
items.summary: Waymo has acquired a massive 5,500-acre proving ground in Arizona owned by Route 14 Investment Partners LLC, a Delaware shell company associated with Apple, according to documents filed with Maricopa County.

items.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
items.summary: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

items.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
items.summary: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its longstanding Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

items.title: Pentagon says Alibaba, Baidu, BYD, and Unitree support China’s military
items.summary: The Trump administration released the updated version of the list four months ago and then quickly pulled it without explaining why

items.title: Apple just taught your iPhone to finish your sentences, your photos, and your workflows
items.summary: Apple is adding new AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps.

items.title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
items.summary: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade, letting you describe the workflow you want in a prompt.

items.title: Apple’s Image Playground doesn’t suck anymore
items.summary: Apple's AI image generator is getting a makeover that could make it more competitive.

items.title: Apple’s Photos app is getting new AI editing features
items.summary: A new spatial "Reframe" feature will let users use AI to adjust perspectives.

items.title: Apple gives Siri its own dedicated app
items.summary: Siri is finally getting its own app.

items.title: Apple is fixing the headache of splitting the bill with its new Siri in Camera feature
items.summary: "If you're grabbing a bite with friends and point your iPhone at the bill, then [you can] select what you ordered to split the tab with Apple Cash," said Apple VP of Software Sebastien Marineau-Mes.

items.title: Apple puts parents back in control of kids’ iPhone use
items.summary: Apple is putting control back into the hands of parents with more granular screen time features.
